comment.body:
Ya definitely fair. And I understand that trading signals isn’t your intent. But, letting us see how you implement the data is helpful. Also, for those of you who say “it’s up to you to use the data and make your own decisions”, fair point, but it’s still vitally important to have a “proof of concept”. You definitely executed that yesterday Alma and congrats on the excellent call.

You are juggling a lot, and being a fellow creative thinker, I can understand how things slip through the cracks and how you might not be running things like a Toyota factory. But there is a balance, and you understand where I’m coming from there. Hence why I try to give feedback to help with the caveat that it’s always easier said than done.

In terms of calling your change of outlook in March OPEX “dramatic”, this emphasizes the importance of choosing words carefully. I believe we had a very bullish sentiment going into March expo and post NVDA earnings, as evidenced by when you entered those calls last week.

Now, we got those weaselly pros sending us on the stairway to hell while they eat thirsty bears alive like a Komodo Dragon, with high put premiums that will be hard to realize profit on, along with fomo bulls who will see their intermediate/longer term term calls expire at zero. Nobody will be making money on a swing/intermediate basis except those that are shorting straight shares. Or short term degens like us who will snipe in and out, in favor of bounces on the call side.

So I don’t think “dramatic” is too dramatic 😜. But maybe so lol. Either way, it’s different.

Thanks for responding, taking my feedback with consideration, and being a kind listener.

comment.body:
Yo, pay attention for a sec..especially those with less experience.
Sometimes Alma doesn’t give exact timing, and that’s because sometimes you just can’t. And there’s nothing worse than someone saying “Wednesday”, only for the move to actually happen on Tuesday.
What Alma does is exactly what every good quant in the industry should be doing..she lays out the environment, explains cause and effect, and shows where transitions happen.
This picture gets sharper over time, but you already know what to look for.
So the scenario is mapped out, along with its invalidations (though yeah, Alma tends to be pretty straightforward with her bias), and this scenario keeps getting clearer as time progresses.
There are very few moments when you can say with pinpoint accuracy what’s going to happen and when.
Last week’s Thursday reversion call was one of those moments..I could tell Alma was 100% sure. The logic was airtight.
But it’s not always that clean, especially when there’s a ton of 0DTE activity

comment.body:
Ya definitely fair. And I understand that trading signals isn’t your intent. But, letting us see how you implement the data is helpful. Also, for those of you who say “it’s up to you to use the data and make your own decisions”, fair point, but it’s still vitally important to have a “proof of concept”. You definitely executed that yesterday Alma and congrats on the excellent call.

You are juggling a lot, and being a fellow creative thinker, I can understand how things slip through the cracks and how you might not be running things like a Toyota factory. But there is a balance, and you understand where I’m coming from there. Hence why I try to give feedback to help with the caveat that it’s always easier said than done.

In terms of calling your change of outlook in March OPEX “dramatic”, this emphasizes the importance of choosing words carefully. I believe we had a very bullish sentiment going into March expo and post NVDA earnings, as evidenced by when you entered those calls last week.

Now, we got those weaselly pros sending us on the stairway to hell while they eat thirsty bears alive like a Komodo Dragon, with high put premiums that will be hard to realize profit on, along with fomo bulls who will see their intermediate/longer term term calls expire at zero. Nobody will be making money on a swing/intermediate basis except those that are shorting straight shares. Or short term degens like us who will snipe in and out, in favor of bounces on the call side.

So I don’t think “dramatic” is too dramatic 😜. But maybe so lol. Either way, it’s different.

Thanks for responding, taking my feedback with consideration, and being a kind listener.

comment.body:
I am joining this chat class today, with the intention of seeking solutions rather than speaking in vain. Personally, I really like the threads that Alma creates every day, but sometimes newcomers like me miss information because people interrupt when she is speaking, and the thread gets fragmented. My idea would be:  

1. A channel with chat disabled on weekends for questions and answers that arose during the week or at the end of the day (as Alma sees fit).  
2. A channel with chat disabled for the threads that she deems necessary, so no one interrupts and the information remains visible to everyone. This way, Alma gets less exhausted, and we all benefit from having the information organized. (For these channels, the image she already has for Q&A is ready; we just need one that says "Thread" or simply the 🧵 emoji.)  
3. Another idea would be to try to answer questions within that message NOT using the reply option but instead using the alternative "REPLY IN A NEW SUBTHREAD." This way, I believe there would be fewer messages throughout the day, and the answers would remain within the same question or inquiry messages.  

Sorry for the long text.

Here's the Trading view script for the options levels. You have to update it manually each morning based on her narrative. I use chat GPT to do it for me.  //@version=5
indicator("GEX and Vanna Levels", overlay=true)


// Ensure the script only runs on SPX
isSPX = syminfo.tickerid == "SP:SPX" or syminfo.tickerid == "SPX" or syminfo.tickerid == "SPX500USD"


// User settings to toggle individual elements
showButterflySpread  = input(true, title="Show Butterfly Spread 5885/5965/6050")
showCentroid         = input(true, title="Show Centroid 5965.94")
showDownsideGamma    = input(true, title="Show Downside Gamma 5925.33")
showZeroVanna        = input(true, title="Show Zero Vanna 5988.5")


// Define key levels from the narrative
butterfly_lower      = 5885.0
butterfly_centroid   = 5965.94
butterfly_upper      = 6050.0


downside_gamma       = 5925.33
zero_vanna           = 5988.5


// Colors and label settings
centroid_color      = color.green
downside_color      = color.red
zero_vanna_color    = color.orange
transparent_bg      = color.new(color.gray, 10)
label_size          = size.normal
label_style         = label.style_none


// Plot horizontal dashed lines for each level if toggled
hline_centroid = hline(showCentroid ? butterfly_centroid : na, "Centroid 5965.94", showCentroid ? centroid_color : na, hline.style_dashed, 2)
hline_downside = hline(showDownsideGamma ? downside_gamma : na, "Downside Gamma 5925.33", showDownsideGamma ? downside_color : na, hline.style_dashed, 2)
hline_zero_vanna = hline(showZeroVanna ? zero_vanna : na, "Zero Vanna 5988.5", showZeroVanna ? zero_vanna_color : na, hline.style_dashed, 2)


// Detect the first and last bar of the current trading day
var int first_bar_of_day = na
var int last_bar_of_day  = na
first_bar_of_day := ta.change(dayofweek) ? bar_index : nz(first_bar_of_day)
last_bar_of_day  := first_bar_of_day + 390  // Approximate full trading session


// Draw butterfly spread horizontal box for the current trading day if toggled
var box butterflyBox = na
if isSPX and bar_index == first_bar_of_day and showButterflySpread
    butterflyBox := box.new(first_bar_of_day, butterfly_upper, last_bar_of_day, butterfly_lower, xloc=xloc.bar_index, border_color=color.new(color.gray, 0), border_width=1, bgcolor=color.new(color.gray, 90))


// Create labels for each level if toggled, only on the current trading day
var label centroidLabel = na
var label downsideGammaLabel = na
var label zeroVannaLabel = na


if isSPX and bar_index >= first_bar_of_day and bar_index <= last_bar_of_day
    if showCentroid
        centroidLabel := label.new(first_bar_of_day, butterfly_centroid, "Centroid 5965.94", xloc=xloc.bar_index, color=transparent_bg, size=label_size, style=label_style, textcolor=centroid_color, textalign=text.align_center)
    if showDownsideGamma
        downsideGammaLabel := label.new(first_bar_of_day, downside_gamma, "Downside Gamma 5925.33", xloc=xloc.bar_index, color=transparent_bg, size=label_size, style=label_style, textcolor=downside_color, textalign=text.align_center)
    if showZeroVanna
        zeroVannaLabel := label.new(first_bar_of_day, zero_vanna, "Zero Vanna 5988.5", xloc=xloc.bar_index, color=transparent_bg, size=label_size, style=label_style, textcolor=zero_vanna_color, textalign=text.align_center)


// Alert for a potential breakthrough of the Zero Vanna level
alertcondition(close > zero_vanna, title="Zero Vanna Breakthrough", message="Price breaking above Zero Vanna 5988.5 may indicate volatility contraction")

comment.body: Here's the Chat GPT prompt that I used today: Update the script with this narrative. Be sure to only include those levels and descriptions in the narrative below. Also replace the Iron Condor with the butterfly spread mentioned. "We can see an obvious long fly spread at 5885/5965/6050The 5965 is fully aligns with my coded 5965.94 as the centroid point, kind of a turning point: below the downside gamma magnetic effect kicks in, and so does the upside gamma bove the line.Coding 5925.33 the lvl where the longer downside gamma starts to affect the price by swallowing volatility, and slowing the momentum down by increasing repel. This can push the price back temporary towards the centroid.I code zero vanna at 5988.5, vol should decrease to break through this lvl (as MMs have net positive vanna convexity), but charm convexity is negativ, so into the PM gamma will decrease, below this region giving more room for volatility."
